title: Creamy Smoky German Bramley Apple Gratin
description: A comforting solo‑dinner gratin that balances the tartness of bramley apples with a smoky, creamy sauce and a crisp parmesan topping.
instructions: Blanch the apple slices in a pot of boiling salted water for 2 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter with the olive oil over medium heat; add the smoked paprika and thyme, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the heavy cream and simmer gently, stirring until the sauce thickens slightly (about 3–4 minutes). Season with a pinch of salt.
Arrange the blanched apple slices in a single layer in a baking dish. Pour the creamy sauce over the apples, ensuring even coverage.
Sprinkle the grated Parmesan cheese evenly across the top of the dish.
Bake uncovered for 25 minutes, or until the sauce is bubbling and the cheese has turned golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let the gratin rest for 5 minutes to set.
Serve warm, optionally garnished with fresh thyme or a light drizzle of smoked olive oil.
